DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS RANJIT BIRAK, PARMINDER BIRAK, AND PREM SINGH'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' demurrer to the first cause of action for "fraud" is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Intentional misrepresentation and concealment are two distinct causes of action even though they are both species of fraud. (See CACI 1900 [elements of intentional misrepresentation]; CACI 1901 [elements of concealment]; Hoffman v. 162 North Wolfe LLC (2014) 228 Cal.App.4th 1178 [reviewing summary adjudication ruling of "two fraud claims" and examining the concealment and intentional misrepresentation claims separately].) Plaintiffs must segregate these distinct claims and plead them separately. (See Cal. R. Ct. 2.112 [causes of action must be separately stated].)

As plaintiffs correctly note, a concealment/nondisclosure claim often can be pleaded with less specificity based on the nature of the claim. (See Alfaro v. Community Housing Imp. System & Planning Ass'n., Inc. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1356, 1384; Jones v. ConocoPhillips (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 1187, 1199.) An intentional misrepresentation claim needs to be pleaded with specificity, but plaintiffs may allege that defendants are liable for Mrs. Singh's statements/nondisclosures if all of the defendants were acting in concert. (See Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 511 ["By participation in a civil conspiracy, a coconspirator effectively adopts as his or her own the torts of other coconspirators within the ambit of the conspiracy. [citation] In this way, a coconspirator incurs tort liability co-equal with the immediate tortfeasors."].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
